# SwissCapital.news Editorial Doctrine

These rules guide editorial decisions and agent behavior. They are **system doctrine**, not published content.

## Publishing Philosophy

- **We publish selectively.** Archive depth matters more than publishing cadence.
- **It is better to release a small number of durable strategic essays than to simulate momentum through generic volume.**
- Quality gates over quantity. Every article must pass sourcing and credibility checks before publication.

## Credibility Standards

- **Authorship:** Institutional bylines only (SwissCapital Research, Editorial Desk, etc.). No invented personas.
- **Sourcing:** All strategic claims must be grounded in named examples, public filings, policy documents, or official data.
- **Datebase:** Publication dates should reflect archive depth. Back-date to reflect established publication history if appropriate.
- **References:** Every flagship article must include a references section with inspectable sources.

## When These Rules Apply

Use this doctrine when:
- Reviewing article submissions for publication
- Making authorship or byline decisions
- Setting editorial workflow gates (accept/revise/reject)
- Evaluating AI-generated or draft content for publication readiness
- Planning content calendar and publishing pace

## Anti-Patterns (Never Do)

- ✗ Invent individual analyst personas with fake credentials
- ✗ Publish thin articles (< 1,500 words) with unverified claims
- ✗ Use plausible-but-unverifiable sources
- ✗ Prioritize publishing frequency over essay quality
